title: lastrea heineri
intro: Lastrea heineri, commonly known as the Heiner's fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The Heiner's fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. The plant's therapeutic actions include its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of the Heiner's fern include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Lastrea heineri can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, nausea, and vomiting. In extreme cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to liver damage and kidney failure. Additionally, the plant's high levels of certain compounds may cause dizziness, headaches, and irregular heartbeats in susceptible individuals. Improper use of Lastrea heineri can also lead to interactions with other medications, potentially resulting in adverse effects.
